summary: v1 F1 (PLang --test regression 206→204 after canonicalization) is closed. Coder's bfb34bca4 added App.Parent and made IsInRoot walk the parent chain, so a child app (per-test child spun up by test.run) inherits its parent's filesystem scope. The walk runs on Absolute, so F1's canonicalization protection is intact — widening the root set doesn't widen what gets compared. Codeanalyzer v2 caught the cycle-DoS risk on a public-settable Parent (N1) and a bare catch in Canonicalize (N2); coder addressed both in ecdd0de4f (depth cap at 16; try/catch removed entirely so GetFullPath exceptions escape loud). Clean rebuild: C# 3031/3031, PLang 206/206 (matches runtime2 baseline). PLNG002 has exactly two file-scope carve-outs, both inside Plng002.cs; zero pragma/SuppressMessage/NoWarn/editorconfig suppressions anywhere else in the tree. Branch is ready for docs.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: agree — v2 findings accurate; N1+N2 closures sound (coder's removal of the bare catch is stronger than codeanalyzer's filtered-catch suggestion); N3/N4/N5 correctly deferred as latency/cosmetic.
previous_reviews_assessed.coder: agree — App.Parent is the minimal correct fix; cycle cap reasonable; bare catch removal stronger than codeanalyzer asked.
previous_reviews_assessed.security: agree — F1 still closed (parent walk uses canonicalized Absolute), F2/F3 unchanged, no new attack surface (Parent is settable but action handlers have no reach to attacker-controlled app refs).

v1_findings_status.F1_plang_suite_regression: closed — bfb34bca4 added App.Parent chain in IsInRoot; clean-rebuild full-suite plang --test reproduces 206/206 same as runtime2 baseline; the 2 CompileLlmNotes tests pass.
